full_prompt: Design a low-cost neonatal incubator chassis targeting low- and middle-income-country clinical deployment. The device must maintain a neonate thermal environment of 32.0 to 37.0 C +/- 0.5 C within an enclosed 720 mm x 370 mm x 300 mm infant compartment (PMMA canopy on structural chassis) against a worst-case ambient of 20 C, with a target parts cost under $400 per unit. The structural chassis is a single welded carbon steel powder-coated frame 860 mm x 500 mm x 1100 mm tall, supported on four 75 mm lockable casters, and must carry a payload of 5.0 kg (neonate + mattress + bedding) plus an integrated 15 kg heater/humidifier module plus a 1.0 kg O2 sensor array, all operating concurrently. The chassis must simultaneously satisfy these pass/fail criteria: (R1) maximum von Mises stress in the steel frame under 1.0 g vertical plus 0.3 g lateral IEC 60601-1 transport load combination with 21 kg payload must not exceed 167 MPa (yield 250 MPa / SF 1.5 per IEC 60601-1 Clause 9), (R2) chassis top-rail deflection under the full 21 kg payload must not exceed 2 mm to protect the PMMA canopy seal, (R3) thermal steady-state analysis must show that a 100 W convective heater can maintain 35 C +/- 0.5 C inside the 80 L infant compartment against a 20 C ambient, (R4) device tip-over stability angle must be at least 10 degrees per IEC 60601-1 Clause 9.4, and (R5) the chassis must withstand a linear buckling analysis under 4x combined payload with a first-mode load factor of at least 2.0. Thermal analysis is required for R3. Vibration, fluid CFD, and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver a STEP AP242 assembly of the chassis + casters + canopy interface plus a design history file entry listing maximum von Mises stress, top-rail deflection, steady-state compartment temperature with rise time, tip-over stability angle, and first buckling load factor.
short_prompt:
Design a low-cost neonatal incubator chassis targeting LMIC clinical deployment. The chassis is a welded powder-coated carbon steel frame of roughly 860 x 500 x 1100 mm on four 75 mm lockable casters, enclosing a 720 x 370 x 300 mm infant compartment under a PMMA canopy. It must carry a 5 kg neonate/bedding load plus a 15 kg heater/humidifier module plus a 1 kg O2 sensor array, keeping BOM cost under $400.

Design to IEC 60601-1 (general medical electrical safety) and IEC 60601-2-19 (incubator thermal performance). Chassis base is seated on the four casters on a rigid floor.

Load cases.
- LC1 transport vector: 1.0 g vertical + 0.3 g lateral simultaneously with 21 kg payload.
- LC2 thermal steady state: maintain 35 C +/- 0.5 C in the ~80 L infant compartment against 20 C ambient with a 100 W convective heater.
- LC3 tip-over stability: tilt the device per IEC 60601-1 Clause 9.4.
- LC4 buckling: 4x combined payload (84 kg) on the chassis.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Parts-cost ceiling ($400) is a BOM rollup, not an FEA.

prompt.title: Low-cost neonatal incubator chassis (LMIC)
prompt.brief: Design a welded steel neonatal incubator chassis (860 x 500 x 1100 mm) with lockable casters, 21 kg payload, 100 W heater maintaining 35 C +/- 0.5 C in 80 L compartment, meeting IEC 60601-1/2-19 and a $400 BOM ceiling.

material.frame: Carbon steel, yield 250 MPa, E 200 GPa, powder-coated

boundary_conditions.heater: internal convective at top of compartment

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: beam/shell for chassis, solid for casters

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Transport random vibration is outside the pass/fail scope.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Compartment heat is lumped; no explicit CFD required.
exclusions_explained.radiation_analysis: Phototherapy is out of scope for the chassis brief.

out_of_scope_but_real_world_needed: Humidity and oxygen concentration feedback control

eval_coverage: BOM cost ceiling as LMIC requirement
